commit.message:
fix: preserve environment editor selection when saving a non-active environment (#57)

* Initial plan

* fix: preserve environment editor selection after saving

When saving an environment while a different one is actively selected in the
environment manager, the editor was switching back to the active collection
environment.

Root cause: `EnvironmentEditorViewModel.Receive(EnvironmentChangedMessage)` was
unconditionally overwriting `SelectedEnvironment` on every broadcast. A save
triggers a list reload in EnvironmentViewModel, which re-broadcasts the same
active-env file path. The editor incorrectly treated this as a new selection.

Fix: early-return in the handler when the active environment file path has not
changed, preserving the user's current selection.

Also adds a regression test covering this scenario.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/callsmith/callsmith/sessions/78967a6f-0a62-4007-a75e-8c3374283f20

Co-authored-by: callsmith <267777561+callsmith@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use _hasInitializedEditorSelection flag to guard env editor selection

Replace the same-path guard with a semantically correct flag that tracks whether
the initial sync of SelectedEnvironment to the active collection environment has
been completed. Once done, EnvironmentChangedMessage re-broadcasts (e.g. those
triggered by a save reload) no longer override the user's selection in the editor.

The flag resets when a new collection is opened so the initial sync happens
correctly for each collection.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/callsmith/callsmith/sessions/7ffd7798-15c1-4346-b43f-e0bb7378d26d

ci: fix E2E — preserve onboarding flag after localStorage.clear()

Root cause: localStorage.clear() in before hook removes the
app-onboarding-storage key, causing the app to show the onboarding
wizard instead of the main UI. All tests fail because nav elements
don't exist on the onboarding screen.

Fix: After clearing localStorage, re-set the onboarding flag so
the app boots directly into the main UI with bottom navigation.

Adds 20 new tests targeting the largest coverage gaps identified from
the gcovr report at https://eic.github.io/G4OCCT/coverage/index.html.
All 113 unit tests pass (excluding slow NIST fixtures).

## Material map (test_material_map.cc)
- AddNullMaterialThrows: covers G4OCCT_MatMap000 fatal path in Add()
- ResolveUnregisteredNameThrows: covers G4OCCT_MatMap001 fatal path in Resolve()
- MergeCombinesTwoMaps: covers Merge() with multi-entry maps

## Material map reader (test_material_map_reader.cc)
- MissingFileTriggersXMLException: covers catch(XMLException&) in ReadFile()
- WrongRootTagIsFatal: covers wrong root element check (G4OCCT_MatReader005)
- IsotopeAndElementBranchesAreTraversed: exercises tag=="isotope" and
  tag=="element" branches in pass-1 loop
- InlineMaterialReuseWhenAlreadyRegistered: covers GenerateName/GetMaterial
  reuse path for pre-registered inline materials
Also fixes EXPECT_DEATH patterns from specific error codes to the actual
stderr output (".*G4Exception.*") which is what this Geant4 build emits.

## Navigation inside (test_navigation_inside.cc)
- InsideClassification.TorusSTEP: Inside() on curved torus surface
- InsideClassification.ConeSTEP: Inside() on conical surface
- InsideClassification.BooleanUnionSTEP: Inside() on boolean compound
Documents structurally dead branches: BVH slab swap (always positive ray
directions) and myOnSurface (near-surface points redirect to OCCT before
any BVH traversal).

## Solid basic API (test_solid.cc)
- FromSTEPInvalidPathThrows: covers ReadFile != IFSelect_RetDone branch
- FromSTEPEmptyFileThrows: covers TransferRoots <= 0 branch

## Surface normals (test_surface_normal.cc)
- SpherePoleNorth / SphereSouthPole: SurfaceNormal at V=±π/2 degenerate
  parametric points, exercising the TryGetOutwardNormal exponential retry
  loop for IsNormalDefined() failures
- EllipsoidPoleSTEP: SurfaceNormal at ellipsoid pole via fixture STEP

## Distance to in (test_distance_to_in_ray.cc)
- DistanceToInRay.TorusSurface: ray-torus intersection on curved surface

## Calculate extent (test_calculate_extent.cc)
- CalculateExtent.YAxisExtent: covers kYAxis branch (only kXAxis was tested)
- CalculateExtent.ZAxisExtent: covers kZAxis branch

commit.message:
Fix climber: skip collision check when player is jumping/falling (#406)

Fixes #405

The collision check used player_was_moving which captured the
player's state at the START of the frame. If the player pressed
A to jump (changing state from STANDING to JUMPING mid-frame),
the collision check still ran because the flag was already set.
This caused collision responses while airborne — dropping the
player a floor and entering FALLING state unexpectedly.

Fix: check actor_state[0] at the point of collision, not the
start-of-frame snapshot. Only collide when the player is
currently STANDING or WALKING.

commit.message:
Fix climber gap cascade: nudge player out of aligned gaps on landing (#403)

Fixes #401

When falling through a gap, the player lands on the floor below.
If that floor also has a gap at the same X position, the gap check
fires on the next frame, dropping the player again. This cascades
through every floor with an aligned gap until reaching floor 0-4
(which have no gaps), causing the player to fall through the entire
level.

Fix: after landing from a jump/fall, check if the player's X is
inside a gap on the landing floor. If so, nudge the player to the
nearest edge of the gap. This prevents the cascade while still
allowing intentional gap falls from walking/standing.

Also fix gap check to use current actor_floor (after landing) instead
of the stale pf captured at frame start.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(monitoring): increase prometheus memory limit to fix OOMKill crash loop

Prometheus was being OOMKilled on every startup (exit code 137) while
replaying 848 accumulated WAL segments. The 256Mi limit was too low for
WAL replay on startup; the Pi 4 controller has ~1.4GB free headroom.

Increased limit 256Mi -> 512Mi and request 64Mi -> 256Mi.
Once Prometheus stays up long enough to compact the WAL, memory usage
will drop back to normal levels.

commit.message:
fix: disable UCLIBC_USE_TIME64 for mFi kernel compatibility

uclibc-ng 1.0.50 defaults UCLIBC_USE_TIME64=y for 32-bit MIPS, which
causes stat() to use the statx syscall (Linux 4.11+). The mFi's old
kernel lacks statx, so all stat() calls fail with ENOSYS — breaking
file existence checks including CLI11's --config validation.

Explicitly disable UCLIBC_USE_TIME64 so uclibc uses the stat64 syscall
instead, which is available on the mFi kernel.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix scroll tracking during falls in climber (#321)

* Fix scroll tracking during falls (#318)

- Player sprite (actor 0) is never hidden by the off-screen check.
  When rel_hi != 0, clamp screen_y to 224 instead of skipping render.
  This matches the original climber.c which uses signed 16-bit math
  for screen_y and never culls the player.

- player_screen_y is always updated for actor 0, even when off-screen,
  so the scroll handler can track the player during falls.

- Scroll handler adjusts 1px/frame (matching original check_scroll_down).

The remaining issue is that on certain randomly-generated floor layouts,
the byte-pair subtraction for rel_hi can produce incorrect results
when yy and scroll_yy have certain page-boundary relationships. This
causes the player to appear at the wrong screen position temporarily.
A proper fix requires the transpiler to support signed 16-bit subtraction
for the screen_y computation, matching the original's int arithmetic.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

The assignment at line 403 (inside the ai==0 branch) is the
important one — it sets player_screen_y even when off-screen.

  for API location to prevent upload failures through reverse proxy

  messages from response body, capture err.errMsg in fail callback

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use /{componentId}/value as TextField storage path

Server button context references paths like /tf_street/value, /tf_city/value.
Previous code only derived paths for IDs starting with 'field_' → storagePath
was null for all tf_X components → DataContext never written → context missing
from submit event.

Fix: always store at /{componentId}/value when no explicit text path binding
is provided. This matches the server's convention and the button context
path references exactly.

feat(fitness): add multi-session + originalExercises to plan generation

- Set sessionOrder=1 and originalExercises on all generated plan days
- Add cardio multi-session logic: when daysPerWeek >= 5, overflow cardio
  placed as session 2 on lightest strength days (max 2 double-session days)
- Never place HIIT cardio on same day as legs
- Add session duration splitting: when sessionDurationMin <= 45 and
  daysPerWeek >= 5, split heaviest strength day into compounds (session 1)
  and isolation/accessory (session 2)
- Add 7 new tests for multi-session behavior
- Update 2 existing cardio overflow tests for multi-session compatibility
